title: Listening - Part 4: Teaching Yoga
description: You will hear part of a radio interview with a woman called Yvonne, who is a yoga instructor. For questions 24–30, choose the correct answer (A, B or C).

content.audio_script: Presenter: My guest this afternoon is Yvonne Price, who teaches yoga. Yvonne, what first took you to a yoga class? || Yvonne: I had been sitting at a desk all day while working in a school office, and my back regularly hurt. I was not interested in yoga at first, but a colleague kept recommending it. [24]After just a few classes, my back felt noticeably better, so I continued going[/24]. I liked the calm atmosphere too, which surprised me. || Presenter: How did you make the move from student to instructor? || Yvonne: I later trained as a primary-school teacher and was still teaching when I qualified in yoga. At first, I ran one evening session a week. Then numbers grew and a local sports centre offered me more classes. [25]I left my teaching job only after there was enough regular yoga work to support me[/25]. It was a careful decision rather than a sudden change. || Presenter: What do you enjoy most about teaching yoga? || Yvonne: It is satisfying when somebody becomes stronger, but that happens gradually. The moment I most enjoy is earlier: [26]seeing beginners arrive uncertain and then discover that they can take part successfully[/26]. Many believe they are too stiff or unfit, and it is lovely to change that view. || Presenter: Has yoga affected your own health apart from your back? || Yvonne: Yes. I used to lie awake thinking about work, and I often felt tired the next day. The breathing exercises have been especially valuable. [27]Since I began practising regularly, I find it much easier to relax at night and sleep well[/27]. I still have busy days, but I deal with them better. || Presenter: Do you give students general lifestyle advice? || Yvonne: Only when they ask. I do remind them that one class cannot solve everything. You need time to rest, relationships that make you feel good, and work that does not take over your life. [28]In particular, I encourage people to notice when their daily routine leaves no time for recovery[/28]. Balance is more realistic than perfection. || Presenter: What about healthy eating? || Yvonne: There are plenty of fashionable rules online. I’m not qualified to tell people exactly what to eat. But people often eat while answering emails or rushing out. [29]Taking time to sit down and pay attention to a meal is a simple improvement many people can make[/29]. It may help them notice when they are full as well. || Presenter: Where should someone go for good advice about yoga? || Yvonne: Friends can recommend a class, and videos are useful for ideas, but beginners need safe guidance. [30]A properly trained instructor can adapt exercises to someone’s body and correct movements before they cause pain[/30]. So I would start in a class, then practise independently once you understand the basics.

task.title: Part 4
task.instruction: For questions 24–30, choose the correct answer (A, B or C).

questions.question: What does Yvonne say about her first yoga classes?

options.A: They improved a physical problem she had.
options.B: She attended them because she already enjoyed exercise.
options.C: They were less relaxing than she had expected.

questions.explanation: Yvonne says her back felt noticeably better after only a few classes.

questions.question: When did Yvonne become a full-time yoga instructor?

options.A: As soon as she had completed her qualification.
options.B: Once she had sufficient regular teaching work.
options.C: After the school where she worked introduced yoga.

questions.explanation: She left her school teaching job after regular yoga work was enough to support her.

questions.question: What does Yvonne like most about being an instructor?

options.B: improving the fitness of experienced students
options.C: showing new students that yoga is possible for them

questions.explanation: Her favourite moment is when uncertain beginners realise they can participate successfully.

questions.question: How has Yvonne's health benefited from yoga?

options.B: She rarely feels tired after work.
options.C: She no longer experiences any pain.

questions.explanation: She says regular practice has made it easier to relax at night and sleep well.

questions.question: What advice does Yvonne give about lifestyle?

options.A: Spend more free time exercising with friends.
options.B: Avoid jobs that involve sitting for long periods.
options.C: Make sure routines include time to rest.

questions.explanation: She encourages people to notice when their routine leaves no time for recovery.

questions.question: What does Yvonne say about healthy eating?

options.A: People should give meals their full attention.
options.B: Online diets are helpful when followed carefully.
options.C: Eating smaller meals is the easiest improvement.

questions.explanation: She recommends sitting down and paying attention while eating.

questions.question: According to Yvonne, beginners should get yoga advice from

options.A: a friend who regularly attends classes.

questions.explanation: Yvonne says a trained instructor can adapt exercises and correct unsafe movements.
